page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and it operates without a GPU.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2692.17 in stress-ng tests. Multi-core CPU performance falls into the average tier with a stress-ng score of 43102.09. Cryptographic capabilities are notable, with the passmark CPU encryption test registering a strong score of 34366.67, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instruction sets. Large-block memory read bandwidth reaches 117.34 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is measured at 63.89 GB/sec. For small-model language tasks, the system achieves a strong text generation speed of 298.13 tokens/sec using a 135M parameter model.

The n2d-highcpu-32 offers high compute density relative to its memory footprint, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for CPU-bound workloads that do not demand large RAM capacities. However, the 1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU represents a significant tradeoff for memory-intensive applications, and the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage. This server is best suited for compute-heavy tasks such as software compilation, text and image processing, static web serving, and cryptographic operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance provides a lean 1.0 GB of memory per vCPU. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance and strong cryptographic execution, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. The server is qualitatively cost-efficient for CPU-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints, though the low RAM-to-vCPU ratio and absence of local storage are key architectural tradeoffs. It is suited for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, text processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, delivering strong single-core and cryptographic performance.

tagline: A compute-dense x86_64 server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and strong single-core performance for CPU-bound workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing a 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio
